name: Axiom MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Axiom MCP server enabling AI agents to query and analyze log data stored in Axiom — running APL (Axiom Processing Language) queries, exploring datasets, and analyzing observability data at scale.
use_cases: Querying application logs with APL from incident response agents
Analyzing log patterns and anomalies for security monitoring agents
Running complex log aggregations for reporting agents
Real-time log analysis during deployment rollouts via agents
Querying distributed trace data alongside logs
not_for: Teams not using Axiom for log storage
best_when: An agent needs to query large volumes of structured log data stored in Axiom using its powerful columnar query engine.
avoid_when: You're using Elasticsearch, Loki, or another log backend — use their native MCPs.

auth.notes: API tokens via AXIOM_TOKEN. Org-level access. No fine-grained dataset scopes.

pricing.notes: Very competitive pricing for log analytics. Open source self-hosted option available. Nexus is Axiom's managed cloud.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not prominently documented

known_agent_gotchas: APL (Axiom Processing Language) is Axiom-specific — agents must learn APL syntax
Dataset names must be known — agents need discovery step
Time range is required for efficient queries — always specify _time filter
API token has no dataset-level scopes — full org access
Query results can be large — implement limits and pagination
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API tokens lack dataset-level scopes. SOC 2 Type II, GDPR. Overall adequate security for a log analytics platform.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. No dataset-level scopes is a gap. SOC 2 Type II. Adequate security for developer observability.
